data.title: NIST Allocates Over $3 Million to Small Businesses Advancing AI, Biotechnology, Semiconductors, Quantum and More
data.summary: NIST is allocating funding to eight small businesses in seven states under the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) program.

data.title: NIST Issues Broad Agency Announcement for Proposals to Advance Microelectronics Technologies
data.summary: The CHIPS for America funding opportunity will support critical research, prototyping and commercial solutions that advance microelectronics technology in the United States.

data.title: In Quantum Sensing, What Beats Beating Noise? Meeting Noise Halfway.
data.summary: A team including scientists at NIST may have found a new way of dealing with noise at the microscopic scales where quantum physics reigns.

data.title: NIST and Partners Use Quantum Mechanics to Make a Factory for Random Numbers
data.summary: Broadcast as a free public service, the beacon can be used anywhere an independent source of random numbers would be useful, such as selecting jury candidates or assigning resources through a lottery.

data.title: Novel ‘Quantum Refrigerator’ Is Great at Erasing Quantum Computer’s Chalkboard
data.summary: Quantum computers need a "clean" workspace, and a team including scientists at NIST has found a way to make one.

data.title: 3 NIST Researchers Receive Presidential Rank Awards
data.summary: All three were awarded the “Distinguished Rank,” the highest category of Presidential Rank Awards.

data.title: NIST Awards $15 Million to ASTM International to Establish Standardization Center of Excellence
data.summary: The new center will support U.S. engagement in international standardization for critical and emerging technologies.

data.title: Tiny New Lasers Fill a Long-Standing Gap in the Rainbow of Visible-Light Colors, Opening New Applications
data.summary: Researchers have created orange, yellow and green lasers tiny enough to fit on a chip.

data.title: NIST Participates in White House Summit on Standards for Critical and Emerging Technology
data.summary: A new Implementation Roadmap provides recommendations and actions for putting the U.S. standards strategy into effect.

data.title: The Big Quantum Chill: NIST Scientists Modify Common Lab Refrigerator to Cool Faster With Less Energy
data.summary: The Big Quantum Chill: NIST Scientists Modify Common Lab Refrigerator to Cool Fa...

data.title: New Spin-Squeezing Techniques Let Atoms Work Together for Better Quantum Measurements
data.summary: Opening new possibilities for quantum sensors, atomic clocks and tests of fundamental physics, JILA researchers have developed new ways of “entangling” or interlinking the properties of large numbers of particles.

data.title: NIST Seeks Input on Implementation of National Standards Strategy for Critical and Emerging Technology
data.summary: The strategy promotes technologically sound standards that help American industry compete internationally on a level playing field.

data.title: NIST ‘Toggle Switch’ Can Help Quantum Computers Cut Through the Noise
data.summary: The novel device could lead to more versatile quantum processors with clearer outputs.

data.title: NIST/JILA Physicist Ana Maria Rey Elected to National Academy of Sciences
data.summary: As a theoretical physicist, Ana Maria Rey studies atomic, molecular and optical physics, condensed matter physics and quantum information science.

data.title: NIST’s Grid of Quantum Islands Could Reveal Secrets for Powerful Technologies
data.summary: The grids provided playing fields in which electrons could behave in nearly ideal, textbook-like conditions.

data.title: An Entangled Matter-Wave Interferometer: Now With Double the Spookiness!
data.summary: A team of researchers at JILA has for the first time successfully combined two of the “spookiest” features of quantum mechanics to make a better quantum sensor.

data.title: NIST Physicist Adam Kaufman Wins Breakthrough New Horizons in Physics Prize
data.summary: Kaufman won for his work in advancing the control of atoms and molecules to improve atomic clocks and quantum information processing.

data.title: DC-Area U.S. Government Agencies Announce the Washington Metropolitan Quantum Network Research Consortium, or DC-QNet
data.summary: The group will work to advance quantum network capabilities and leadership.

data.title: Optical Fiber Could Boost Power of Superconducting Quantum Computers
data.summary: The secret to building superconducting quantum computers with massive processing power may be an ordinary telecommunications technology — optical fiber.&nbsp

data.title: Error-Prone Quantum Bits Could Correct Themselves, NIST Physicists Show
